title: Determination of florfenicol and florfenicol amine in fish plasma through HPLC MS/MS
author: Betty San Martín
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.zhdf326
materials: Synergi TM 4 µm fusion RP 80Â 50 x 2.0 mm analytic column

steps.title: 250 µL of each plasma sample...
steps.instruction: 250 µL of each plasma sample were placed in 15 mL Falcon tubes and 500 µL Water Contributed by users and 2 mL Acetone Contributed by users were added.

steps.title: Extraction was carried out according to...
steps.instruction: Extraction was carried out according to the method described by Li et al. (2006), with modifications.

steps.title: The tubes were shaken for 00:10:00...
steps.instruction: The tubes were shaken for 00:10:00 , sonicated for 00:10:00 and centrifuged at 5,000 rpm for 00:05:00

steps.title: Subsequently, the resulting supernatant was transferred...
steps.instruction: Subsequently, the resulting supernatant was transferred to another 15 mL Falcon tube containing 2 mL Dichloromethane Bio Basic Inc. Catalog #DC3600.SIZE.1L , and then shaken, sonicated and centrifuged at 5,000 rpm for the same amount of time as before

steps.title: Then, the upper phase ( Water...
steps.instruction: Then, the upper phase ( Water Contributed by users ) was discarded and the remaining phase evaporated at 40 °C to 50 °C under nitrogen flow.

steps.title: Once completely dry, 1 mL of...
steps.instruction: Once completely dry, 1 mL of mobile phase and 1 mL hexane were used to reconstitute each sample, which then was shaken, sonicated and centrifuged at 5,000 rpm.

steps.title: Finally, 300 µL of the lower...
steps.instruction: Finally, 300 µL of the lower phase were transferred to a vial to be injected into the .

steps.title: The limit of detection (LOD) and...
steps.instruction: The limit of detection (LOD) and the limit of quantification (LOQ) established for both analytes in plasma were 0.1 Parts per Million (PPM) and 0.2 Parts per Million (PPM) , respectively.

steps.title: For instrumental analysis, an LC (Agilent,...
steps.instruction: For instrumental analysis, an LC (Agilent, 1290 infinity series) coupled to a triple quadrupole mass spectrometer (API 5500, ABSCIEX) was used.

steps.title: A Synergi TM 4 µm fusion...
steps.instruction: A Synergi TM 4 µm fusion RP 80Â 50 x 2.0 mm analytic column was used.

steps.title: Analyst 1
steps.instruction: Analyst 1.6.3 and Multiquant 3.0 software was used for equipment management and integration, respectively.

steps.title: Chromatographic separation was performed through a...
steps.instruction: Chromatographic separation was performed through a mobile phase using solvent A: 0.1% of Acetoacetic acid Contributed by users in Water Contributed by users ; and a mobile phase solvent B: 0.1% of Acetoacetic acid Contributed by users in Water Contributed by users / Methanol Contributed by users 1:9 ratio; with a gradient flow of 350 µL min -1 and a gradient elution from 32% up to 68% solvent A in 00:03:00 of 35% phase solvent A, and 75% phase solvent B.

steps.title: Injection volume was 2 µL ;...
steps.instruction: Injection volume was 2 µL ; column oven temperature was set at 37 °C .

steps.title: Prior to determining concentrations in plasma,...
steps.instruction: Prior to determining concentrations in plasma, the analytical method was validated by HPLC MS/MS, according to instructions from the European Commission Decision 2002/657/EC (2002).

steps.title: Precursor ions and the two product...
steps.instruction: Precursor ions and the two product ions were identified for florfenicol and florfenicol amine, respectively.

steps.title: Values for essential parameters were estimated...
steps.instruction: Values for essential parameters were estimated for the validation of the analytical method on plasma: specificity, recovery, repeatability, intralaboratory reproducibility, decision limit (CCα), detection capability (CCβ), and linearity.
